title: Container Delivery, Health, Migration Coordination, and Safe Rollout · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss Go decisions for container delivery, health, migration coordination, and safe rollout and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, compiler, network, XML, PostgreSQL, race, load, accessibility, deployment, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for deployable Go feed-service release unit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pra…
objectives: Build a minimal non-root pinned multi-stage image with exact binary, migrations, certificates, ownership, metadata, SBOM, signature, and vulnerability evidence.
Validate configuration, secrets references, database compatibility, migration state, network policy, budgets, and dependency reachability before serving.
Separate liveness, readiness, startup, degraded ingestion, backlog, database, and maintenance states without exposing secrets or causing restart loops.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Feed Go Progressive Rollout
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go team handles case 5269 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to bind non-root artifacts, startup, health, migration ownership, compatibility, canary, and rollback evidence; untrusted XML and feed content must not cross admission or rendering boundaries; then change one tenant, cursor, reader-state, authorization, cache, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Canary compatible binaries and schema with traffic, worker, freshness, error-budget, rollback, and kill-switch evidence.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Feed Go Progressive Rollout.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for canary compatible binaries and schema with traffic, worker, freshness, error-budget, rollback, and kill-switch evidence. For Feed aggregation and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Feed Go Progressive Rollout, keep Feed Go Progressive Rollout connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
